model_narrative: We must place a set of activities into a set of open facilities. Whenever two activities end up at the same facility, a known interaction cost is incurred for that pair. We decide which facility each activity is assigned to. The objective is to make the total interaction cost as small as possible.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the activities that must each be placed at a facility

sets.name: jj
sets.doc: all candidate facilities

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the facilities that are open and available to host activities; only these may receive an assignment

sets.name: k
sets.doc: an alias of the activities i, used to index the second activity in a pair of activities

params.name: a
params.doc: interaction cost incurred when the first activity and the second activity of a pair are both placed at the same facility; many of these costs are zero, meaning that pair has no interaction

vars.name: xb
vars.doc: 1 if the activity is assigned to the facility, 0 otherwise

vars.name: y
vars.doc: stands for the product of the first activity being assigned to the facility and the second activity being assigned to the same facility; it equals 1 only when both activities of the pair are assigned to that facility

vars.name: z
vars.doc: total interaction cost

constraints.name: xb[i,
constraints.intent: Every activity must be placed at exactly one of the open facilities. For each activity, the assignment over all open facilities adds up to exactly one.

constraints.name: z
constraints.intent: Define the total interaction cost as the interaction cost of each pair of activities multiplied by whether both activities of the pair share a facility, added up over every pair of activities and every open facility, and set the total cost equal to that sum.

constraints.name: a[i,
constraints.intent: Tie the shared-facility indicator for a pair of activities to their two assignments. For each pair of activities and each open facility, the indicator that both are placed at that facility must be at least one whenever both activities are assigned there, and otherwise it is free to stay at zero. Only consider pairs that actually have an interaction cost.

query: We're evaluating putting activities act-2 and act-3 in the same building — say fac-1 — to share equipment. What interaction cost does that pairing run us?
